pg154-p1-s2:
But his
venture wound up in a law suit and bogged down completely.
pg154-p1-s3:
The proceeding was shot through with much hard feeling and
controversy.
pg154-p1:
in his enterprise, he would have been set on his feet financially which, at the time, seemed vitally important. But his
venture wound up in a law suit and bogged down completely. The proceeding was shot through with much hard feeling and
controversy.
pg154-p2-s1: Bitterly discouraged, he found himself in a strange place, discredited and almost broke.
pg154-p2-s2:
Still physically weak, and
sober but a few months, he saw that his predicament was dangerous.
pg154-p2-s3: He wanted so much to talk with someone, but whom?
pg154-p2:
Bitterly discouraged, he found himself in a strange place, discredited and almost broke. Still physically weak, and
sober but a few months, he saw that his predicament was dangerous. He wanted so much to talk with someone, but whom?
pg154-p3-s1: One dismal afternoon he paced a hotel lobby wondering how his bill was to be paid.
pg154-p3-s2:
At one end of the room stood a glass
covered directory of local churches.
pg154-p3-s3: Down the lobby a door opened into an attractive bar.
pg154-p3-s4:
He could see the gay crowd
inside.
pg154-p3-s5: In there he would find companionship and release.
pg154-p3-s6:
Unless he took some drinks, he might not have the courage to
scrape an acquaintance and would have a lonely week-end.
pg154-p3:
One dismal afternoon he paced a hotel lobby wondering how his bill was to be paid. At one end of the room stood a glass
covered directory of local churches. Down the lobby a door opened into an attractive bar. He could see the gay crowd
inside. In there he would find companionship and release. Unless he took some drinks, he might not have the courage to
scrape an acquaintance and would have a lonely week-end.
pg154-p4-s1: Of course he couldn't drink, but why not sit hopefully at a table, a bottle of ginger ale before him?
pg154-p4-s2:
After all, had he
not been sober six months now?
pg154-p4-s3: Perhaps he could handle, say, three drinks - no more!
pg154-p4-s6: Again it was the old, insidious insanity - that first drink.
pg154-p4-s7:
With a shiver, he turned away and walked down the
lobby to the church directory.
pg154-p4-s8: Music and gay chatter still floated to him from the bar.
pg154-p4:
Of course he couldn't drink, but why not sit hopefully at a table, a bottle of ginger ale before him? After all, had he
not been sober six months now? Perhaps he could handle, say, three drinks - no more! Fear gripped him. He was on thin
ice. Again it was the old, insidious insanity - that first drink. With a shiver, he turned away and walked down the
lobby to the church directory. Music and gay chatter still floated to him from the bar.
pg154-p5-s1:
But what about his responsibilities - his family and the men who would die because they would not know how to get well,
ah - yes, those other alcoholics?
pg154-p5-s2: There must be many such in this town.
pg154-p5-s4:
His sanity returned
and he thanked
pg154-p5:
But what about his responsibilities - his family and the men who would die because they would not know how to get well,
ah - yes, those other alcoholics? There must be many such in this town. He would phone a clergyman. His sanity returned
and he thanked
